title: Crispy Winey Light Japanese Bread Claypot Cooked
description: A light and crispy Japanese claypot bread recipe featuring wholegrain bread, perfect for a low-fat friendly meal or snack. Inspired by the flavors of yakitori, this dish is a unique twist on traditional Japanese cuisine.
instructions: Preheat the claypot in the oven to 375°F (190°C) for 10 minutes.
Slice the wholegrain bread into 1-inch thick slices.
In a small bowl, mix the softened unsalted butter with 2 tablespoons of sake (if using).
Place the bread slices in a single layer on the bottom of the claypot.
Brush the bread slices with the butter-sake mixture, making sure to get both sides evenly coated.
Season the bread with a pinch of salt and a sprinkle of black pepper.
Cover the claypot with a lid and place it in the preheated oven.
Bake the bread for 20-25 minutes, or until it is crispy and golden brown on the outside.
Remove the claypot from the oven and let it cool for a few minutes before serving.
Cut the bread into smaller pieces and serve hot, garnished with chopped green onions and a drizzle of soy sauce (if desired).
